name: Pix Rowe Miller's Family Fish Chowder - Katherine Hall Pag
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the bacon in a pan until crispy. Remove it and place on paper towel to drain.
2. In the same pan, sauté the onions with bacon fat until softened.
3. Cut potatoes into half-length slices, then into 1/4 inch pieces. Place in a nonreactive pot large enough for the chowder. Add water to cover the potatoes and bring to a boil until tender. Do not add too much water or the chowder will become soupy.
4. While the potatoes are cooking, cut fish into bite-sized pieces.
5. Once the potatoes are ready, add the fish pieces to the pot. Cover and simmer until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
6. Add the crumbled bacon, sautéed onions, and any remaining grease from the pan. Combine evaporated milk and whole milk. Stir well.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 5 minutes.
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Chowder tastes better when made ahead of time.
VARIATIONS:
- Use olive oil instead of bacon fat.
- Substitute salt pork for bacon.
- Experiment with different combinations of fish: haddock, cod, pollack, monkfish, and hake.
- Garnish with dill, chopped parsley, oyster crackers, or butter.
